model_output:
Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's the comparison between the documented API endpoints in the README and what's actually implemented:

## API Endpoint Comparison

| Endpoint | Method | Documented | Implemented | Status |
|----------|--------|------------|-------------|--------|
| `/api/users` | GET | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | Fully implemented - returns JSON array of users |
| `/api/users` | POST | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | Stub implementation - returns `{"status":"created"}` but doesn't actually parse request body or create users (has TODO comment) |
| `/api/health` | GET | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | Fully implemented - returns "OK" |

## Summary

- **All documented endpoints are implemented** — No undocumented routes exist in the code
- **GET /api/users**: Fully functional, returns hardcoded user data as JSON
- **POST /api/users**: Stub only — the TODO comment in `UsersController.scala` confirms: `"// TODO: parse body, validate, add user"` — it always returns a 201 response without actually processing the request
- **GET /api/health**: Simple implementation that returns plain text "OK" with status 200

The source files show only these two routes are registered in `Main.scala` via:
- `server.addRoute("/api/users", UsersController.handle)`
- `server.addRoute("/api/health", _ => Response(200, "OK"))`
